plugin_desc: TP-Link (JetStream)-specific SNMP collection for switches (TPLINK private MIB, enterprise 11863). In addition to standard IF-MIB interface metrics, it collects TP-Link private OIDs for device health: CPU and memory utilization, both reported directly as percentages. TP-Link SMB switches expose no temperature/fan/power-supply sensors, so those health items are not collected (N/A). Supported brands/models — TP-Link JetStream managed switches (T1500/T1600/T2600 series, etc.).
name: Switch

metrics.name: device_cpu_usage
metrics.description: This metric reports the CPU utilization of the TP-Link (JetStream) device, read per processor from the TPLINK private MIB (tpSysMonitorCpu5Minutes), normalized to a 0-100 percentage. Sustained high CPU usage can indicate control-plane overload, routing instability, or attack traffic, and may lead to slow management response or packet processing delays.

metrics.name: device_memory_usage
metrics.description: This metric reports overall memory utilization of the TP-Link (JetStream) device (TPLINK private MIB tpSysMonitorMemoryUtilization), reported directly as a 0-100 percentage. High memory usage may indicate memory leaks, oversized routing/MAC/ARP tables, or insufficient capacity, and can cause instability or failed allocations.

metrics.name: interface_ifHCInOctets
metrics.description: This metric indicates the average rate of bytes received over the past 5 minutes, measured in bytes per second, from the 64-bit IF-MIB ifHCInOctets counter (ifXTable). The 64-bit counter avoids the rapid wrap of the 32-bit ifInOctets on high-speed switch ports, keeping the rate accurate.

metrics.name: interface_ifHCOutOctets
metrics.description: This metric calculates the average rate of bytes sent over the past 5 minutes, measured in bytes per second, from the 64-bit IF-MIB ifHCOutOctets counter (ifXTable). The 64-bit counter avoids the rapid wrap of the 32-bit ifOutOctets on high-speed switch ports, keeping the rate accurate.
